title: Derris glaziovii
intro: Derris glaziovii, commonly known as Brazilian angel or tropical soap tree, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Derris glaziovii include rotenone, a toxic compound with insecticidal properties, and other alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Derris glaziovii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which may be used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders.
taxonomy: Derris glaziovii is classified under the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. This plant is also a member of the genus Derris, which includes several species of climbing plants native to tropical regions. The classification of Derris glaziovii is further broken down into the order Fabales, class Magnoliopsida, and kingdom Plantae. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and characteristics, such as its ability to fix nitrogen through symbiotic relationships with rhizobia. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Derris glaziovii shares similarities with other legumes in terms of its seed and leaf structure.
uses: Derris glaziovii is known for its various medicinal uses, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its roots and leaves are traditionally used in folk medicine to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. In some cultures, it is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The plant is also believed to have antibacterial properties, making it effective against certain infections. Its medicinal properties are primarily attributed to the presence of various alkaloids and glycosides in its roots and leaves.
benefits: Derris glaziovii, also known as Jupunba, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its root is a rich source of rotenone, a natural insecticide and antiseptic. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in treating conditions such as arthritis and fever. Additionally, it has been used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic. More research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential health benefits.
properties: Derris glaziovii is known to possess several therapeutic properties, primarily due to the presence of rotenone and other rotenoids in its roots. These compounds have been found to exhibit potential as natural insecticides and anti-inflammatory agents. Additionally, Derris glaziovii has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-bacterial properties, helping to treat various skin infections and wounds. Its root extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm these findings. Overall, Derris glaziovii is a valuable plant with a range of therapeutic properties that are still being explored and utilized in modern medicine.
constituents: Derris glaziovii is known to contain active constituents such as rotenone, a toxic compound used as an insecticide, and other related isoflavonoids. The rotenone in Derris glaziovii is primarily responsible for its insecticidal properties and has been used to control various pests. The plant's active constituents have been studied for their potential insecticidal and antimicrobial properties. In addition to rotenone, Derris glaziovii also contains other compounds like desacetylarotenone and desacetylrotenone. These compounds contribute to the plant's overall toxicity and its ability to control insect populations.
parts: Derris glaziovii. The medicinal parts of Derris glaziovii include the roots and stems, which are used to produce rotenone, a natural insecticide and piscicide. side_effects: Derris glaziovii, also known as Angelin or Parai, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The exact side effects of Derris glaziovii are not well documented in available medical literature, but it is known to be toxic to humans and animals if ingested in large quantities.
